steps.text: Perform leak checks during a sampling run.
Perform post-test leak check procedures.
Revise § 63.14 to add ASTM D6784–16 to paragraph (h).
Revise § 63.14 to add 'Standard Methods for the Examination of Waste and Wastewater' method 5210 to paragraph (u).
In § 63.457(c)(4), replace the reference to method 405.1 with reference to method 5210B.
In the appendix to subpart EEE, remove the erroneous language regarding an Interference Response Test in the introductory paragraph of section 5.
In the appendix to subpart EEE, remove section 5.3 in its entirety.
Perform Oxidation Catalyst Efficiency Check (section 11.1.1.3 of this method).

Calculate the desired attenuator value corrected to the emission outlet pathlength using the formula variables: OP1 (Nominal opacity value of required low-, mid-, or high-range calibration attenuators), OP2 (Desired attenuator opacity value from ASTM D6216–20, section 7.5), L1 (Monitoring pathlength), and L2 (Emission outlet pathlength).
Establish appropriate safety and health practices.
Determine the applicable regulatory limitations prior to performing this performance specification.
Consult the CEMS user's manual for specific precautions to be taken with regard to the analytical procedures.
Set the O2 span to 25 percent (or higher if the O2 concentration at the sampling point can be greater than 25 percent).
Set the CO low-range span to 200 ppm and the high range span to 3000 ppm.
Configure the scale for both CEMS to record all readings within a measurement range with a resolution of 0.5 percent.
Operate each monitor in its normal sampling mode as nearly as possible.
Inject the calibration gas into the sample system as close to the sampling probe outlet as practical, ensuring it passes through all CEMS components used during normal sampling.
Challenge the CEMS three non-consecutive times at each measurement point and record the responses.
Carry out the Response Time Test Procedure for both the CO and O2 monitors.
Perform the Relative Accuracy Test Procedure in accordance with PS 2, sections 8.4.3, 8.4.4, and 8.4.5.
Perform concurrent sample collection and analysis, referring to the RM for specific analytical procedures.
Summarize the results on a data sheet as shown in figure 4B–1 of this method.
Calculate the Calibration Error (CE) results for the CO monitor.
Calculate the CE results for the O2 monitor.
Calculate the CERMS Relative Accuracy (RA) expressed as a percentage using equation 2–6 of section 12 of PS 2.
Use method 29, method 30A, or method 30B in appendix A–8 to this part or ASTM Method D6784–16 as the RM for Hg concentration.
Conduct the RM test runs with paired or duplicate sampling systems and use the average of the vapor phase Hg concentrations measured by the two trains.
Obtain data derived with method 4 in appendix A–3 to this part during the RA test.
Conduct a minimum of nine RM test runs.
Report all data including the excluded test run data.
Ensure that the reference gas passes through all filters, scrubbers, conditioners, and other monitor components used during normal sampling.
Pass the reference gas through as much of the sampling probe as practical.
Adjust the CEMS calibration.
Calculate the accuracy for the RAA using equation 1–1 of this method.
Use a single mid- or high-level oxidized Hg (mercuric chloride, HgCl2) reference gas to assess transport and measurement of oxidized mercury.
Determine total gaseous organic matter concentration using Method 25 or 25A of appendix A–7 to 40 CFR part 60.
Use the same test method for both the inlet and outlet measurements.
Conduct inlet and outlet measurements simultaneously.
Submit notice of the intended test method to the Administrator for approval along with notification of the performance test required under § 63.7(b).
Select the sampling port location and the number/location of traverse points at the inlet and outlet of the control device.
Measure the O2 at the inlet and outlet of the control device.
Measure moisture content at the inlet and outlet of the control device as needed to determine CO and O2 concentrations on a dry basis.

Measure moisture content at the inlet and outlet of the control device as needed to determine formaldehyde or THC and O2 concentrations on a dry basis using Method 4 of 40 CFR part 60, appendix A–3, or method 320 of 40 CFR part 63, appendix A, or ASTM D6348–03.
Check gauge calibration quarterly.
Check transducer calibration monthly.

preconditions.text: Ensure compliance with the modified procedures in § 60.534(c) rather than relying solely on ASTM E2515-11 section 9.6.5.1.

If the average emissions for the test are less than 50 percent of the applicable emission standard, you may elect to substitute the applicable emission standard value in the denominator of equation 2–6.
When method 29, method 30B, or ASTM D6784–16 is used.
If the RM and CEMS measure on a different moisture basis.
When method 29, method 30B, or ASTM D6784–16 is used, only test runs for which the paired RM trains meet the relative deviation criteria (RD) of this PS must be used in the RA calculations.
The daily zero (or low-level) CD or the daily high-level CD exceeds two times the limits of the applicable PS’s in appendix B to this part.
At least once every 7 operating days.
If any of the conditions described in paragraphs (e)(1)(vi)(A) through (D) of this section apply to the control device, you must use method 25A.

For CO, O2, and moisture measurement, ducts ≤6 inches in diameter may be sampled at a single point located at the duct centroid and ducts >6 and ≤12 inches in diameter may be sampled at 3 traverse points located at 16.7, 50.0, and 83.3% of the measurement line.

If the duct is >12 inches in diameter and the sampling port location meets the two and half-diameter criterion of section 11.1.1 of method 1 of 40 CFR part 60, appendix A–1, the duct may be sampled at ‘3-point long line’; otherwise, conduct the stratification testing and select sampling points according to section 8.1.2 of method 7E of 40 CFR part 60, appendix A–4.
Measurements to determine O2 concentration must be made at the same time as the measurements for formaldehyde or THC concentration.

The capture system meets the criteria in method 204 of appendix M to 40 CFR part 51 for a permanent total enclosure (PE) and directs all the exhaust gases from the enclosure to an add-on control device.

constraints.text: Do not average particulate matter (PM) results from a non-leaking sampling system with those from a leaking sampling system, as this reduces reported PM emissions and renders the test method inappropriate for compliance determination.
The opacity monitoring equipment must comply with the design specifications of ASTM D6216–20.
The opacity monitor must comply with the manufacturer’s performance specifications of ASTM D6216–20.
This performance specification may involve hazardous materials, operations, and equipment.
This performance specification may not address all of the safety problems associated with its use.
The duration of each gas injection should be sufficient to ensure that the CEMS surfaces are conditioned.
For CO, the CEMS calibration must not drift from the reference value of the calibration standard by more than 3 percent of the span value on either the high or low range.
The mean difference between the CEMS and reference values at all three test points must be no greater than 5 percent of span value for CO monitors and 0.5 percent for O2 monitors.
The response time for the CO or O2 monitor must not exceed 240 seconds.
The RA of the CERMS shall be no greater than 20.0 percent (or 10.0 percent if substituting the emission standard value) in terms of the units of the emission standard.
For method 29 and ASTM Method D6784–16 only, the filterable portion of the sample need not be included when making comparisons to the CEMS results.
For method 29 and ASTM D6784–16, use a minimum sample time of 2 hours and for methods 30A and 30B use a minimum sample time of 30 minutes.

If more than nine sets of RM test runs are performed, RM test run results may be excluded so long as the total number of RM test run results used to determine the CEMS RA is greater than or equal to nine.
The RAA must be calculated in the units of the applicable emission standard.
Must use the procedure described in section 8.3.3 of Performance Specification 12A in appendix B to this part.
Use Method 3 or 3A or 3B of 40 CFR part 60, appendix A–2, or ASTM D6522–00 (Reapproved 2005) (heated probe not necessary).
Measurements to determine O2 must be made at the same time as the measurements for CO concentration.
Use Method 4 of 40 CFR part 60, appendix A–3, or method 320 of 40 CFR part 63, appendix A, or ASTM D6348–03 1 3.
Measurements to determine moisture content must be made at the same time and location as the measurements for CO concentration.
Use a pressure sensor with measurement sensitivity of 0.002 inch water.
